extracted_items.content: Evidence index persists across `/new` command — SQLite connection closes but file stays on disk, allowing new sessions to search previous conversation history

Phase 2 memory improvement: Extraction Decoupling — decouple memory extraction from compaction events, run on schedule (~20 user turns, /new, idle flush), track extraction watermark in session metadata to prevent silent memory loss from failed extraction batches

Phase 2 includes smarter routing: extraction prompt categorizes items as shared (memory graph, all agents) vs agent-specific (memory graph, agent-scoped), stopping shared facts from being dumped into MEMORY.md
Phase 2 includes catch-up before compaction: check if extraction is behind before compaction cuts messages, run catch-up pass to prevent compacting away unextracted content

Phase 3 memory improvement: MEMORY.md Compaction — after extraction runs, if MEMORY.md exceeds 10K tokens, use Haiku to distill to ~5K tokens, keeping only agent-private preferences and active context, removing shared facts that went to the graph

Extraction window design: provide Haiku with context overlap (5 messages before watermark, read-only) plus extraction window (new messages to extract from), explicitly marking zones so Haiku knows what not to extract from
Extraction input token budget capped at ~12K tokens per batch to keep Haiku focused; batch large windows (e.g., 60 messages) into smaller chunks with overlapping context

Extraction deduplication: use deterministic code with cosine similarity thresholds (0.85 min, 0.95 for reinforcement) to match extracted items against memory graph, supporting add/reinforce/supersede/relate actions based on similarity and recency
Extraction watermark only advances on full batch success; on any failure (LLM call, JSON parsing, graph writes), watermark stays put for retry to prevent data loss
Haiku will be given awareness of existing memory graph contents during extraction to provide context about what is already known, reducing noise and improving consistency of extraction decisions

Plan: build test/simulation scripts using Haiku API (similar to Claude code OAuth calling) to test extraction pipeline in isolation before implementing in codebase — run over conversation history (via JSONL), simulate full behavior, test deduplication against memory graph, run fuzz tests and scenarios
